plant_message: I never write TODO comments in code. If something needs doing later, it goes in a GitHub issue with a link in the comment: # See issue #123

plant_message: I prefer explicit over implicit in Python. No star imports, no shadowing builtins, always type-hint function signatures in new code

plant_message: For code reviews I want: one concern per comment, always include a code suggestion, never use the word 'just' (it's condescending)

plant_message: Commit messages must start with a type prefix: feat/fix/refactor/test/docs. Body is mandatory for anything non-trivial. No WIP commits to main

plant_message: I want tests to be DAMP not DRY. Duplicate setup code in tests is fine. Shared test fixtures should only be used when the setup is non-trivial (>10 lines)

plant_message: Pull requests should be under 400 lines changed. If a feature needs more, split into a stack. I will not review PRs over 800 lines as a rule

plant_message: Never swallow exceptions silently. Either re-raise, log at ERROR level with full context, or convert to a domain error. Empty except blocks are a blocking review comment

plant_message: Boolean variables and functions must start with is_, has_, can_, should_. Event handler functions start with on_. Config values are ALL_CAPS constants

plant_message: Branch naming: feature/JIRA-123-short-description, bugfix/JIRA-456-description, hotfix/JIRA-789-description. Always branch off main, never off other feature branches

plant_message: Log lines must be machine-parseable: JSON format with fields timestamp, level, service, trace_id, message. Never log PII. Never log secrets. Use structured logging only

plant_message: I dislike ORMs for complex queries. Use raw SQL with parameterized queries for anything with JOINs. SQLAlchemy core is fine, SQLAlchemy ORM is discouraged

plant_message: REST API response envelope: always {data: ..., error: null} or {data: null, error: {code, message}}. Never return different shapes for success vs error. HTTP status codes must be accurate
